symptoms.symptom_name: Painless progressive jaundice (scleral icterus and cutaneous jaundice)
symptoms.character: deep yellow skin and sclerae, painless, steadily worsening

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless obstructive jaundice is the cardinal presenting feature of malignant distal biliary obstruction.
Stone obstruction is typically painful/colicky rather than painless.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss ~10 kg with anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Significant unexplained weight loss is a cancer alarm feature; with jaundice it strongly supports malignancy.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Courvoisier's law: a palpable non-tender gallbladder with jaundice signals malignant distal obstruction rather than stones. Clinician-only finding — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark urine and pale acholic (greasy) stools

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dark urine (bilirubinuria) plus pale acholic stools confirms an obstructive, post-hepatic pattern.
Pale stools do not occur in pre-hepatic jaundice.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cholestatic pruritus from bile-salt deposition accompanies obstructive jaundice.

symptoms.symptom_name: New-onset atypical diabetes mellitus (non-obese, no family history)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paraneoplastic new-onset diabetes can precede a pancreatic cancer diagnosis, especially in a non-obese older adult.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vague deep epigastric ache radiating to the back
symptoms.character: vague, deep, dull, poorly localised, bores through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep epigastric pain radiating to the back is characteristic of pancreatic and retroperitoneal disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors or right-upper-quadrant peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of Charcot's triad makes active cholangitis unlikely at present, but it remains the emergency to safety-net against.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular over-the-counter paracetamol use for back ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A distractor to consider and exclude; in the context of a strained, obstructed liver, continuing paracetamol adds hepatotoxic risk and should be stopped.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Virchow's node indicates distant metastatic spread. Clinician-only finding — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypodense pancreatic-head mass on multiphase CT

investigation_details.investigation_name: Multiphase contrast-enhanced CT of the pancreas
investigation_details.expected_result: Hypodense mass in the head of the pancreas with distal CBD/pancreatic duct dilatation (double-duct sign)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis and staging not established; biliary decompression delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging demonstrating a pancreatic-head mass with ductal obstruction. Hospital investigation — never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstructive (cholestatic) liver function tests with conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia and coagulopathy

investigation_details.investigation_name: Liver function tests, bilirubin fractionation, coagulation profile
investigation_details.expected_result: Raised bilirubin (conjugated), raised ALP/GGT out of proportion to transaminases, prolonged INR from vitamin-K malabsorption
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity and bleeding risk unquantified before intervention.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Malignant distal biliary obstruction (pancreatic head carcinoma)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cholestatic LFT pattern with coagulopathy confirms obstruction and quantifies pre-procedure risk. Venous bloods — sealed, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Pancreatic head adenocarcinoma with malignant distal CBD obstruction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Painless obstructive jaundice + unintentional weight loss + a palpable non-tender gallbladder (Courvoisier's sign) is the classic ominous constellation of malignant distal biliary obstruction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests, bilirubin, coagulation profile (INR), FBC, renal function, lipase, CA19-9
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm obstructive pattern, quantify bleeding risk before any procedure, and provide a baseline tumour marker. Requires urgent same-day pathology.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal ultrasound (first-line imaging for biliary dilatation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapid, accessible confirmation of biliary/ductal dilatation and possible mass while cross-sectional imaging is arranged.
Definitive characterisation and staging of a pancreatic-head mass; guides biliary decompression.
Patient is afebrile with no cholangitis features; antibiotics are indicated only if sepsis/cholangitis develops and are a hospital decision, not a telehealth action.
